All three re-reviewed. Let me read the fresh qodo verdicts (bug counts) for each.

input.command:
for pr in 41 37 35; do
  echo "===== PR #$pr — latest qodo review ====="
  gh api "repos/kibarik/po-helper/issues/$pr/comments" --jq '[.[] | select(.user.login=="qodo-code-review[bot]")] | last | .body' \
    | grep -iE "Bugs \(|Rule violations|summary>  [0-9]" | head -20
  echo ""
done

  gh api "repos/kibarik/po-helper/issues/$pr/comments" --jq '[.[] | select(.user.login=="qodo-code-review[bot]")] | last | .body' | grep -oE "🐞 Bugs? \([0-9]+\)|No .*issues|🐞 Bug|Rule violations \([0-9]+\)" | head; echo "--- finding titles ---"
  gh api "repos/kibarik/po-helper/issues/$pr/comments" --jq '[.[] | select(.user.login=="qodo-code-review[bot]")] | last | .body' | grep -oE "summary>[[:space:]]+[0-9]+\.[^<]*" | head
done

content.text: qodo **updates** its original review comment in place rather than posting a new one. Let me fetch the updated original review bodies to see current bug counts.

input.command:
for pr in 41 37 35; do
  echo "===== PR #$pr ====="
  # the qodo review comment is the one containing "Code Review by Qodo" header; get the most-reacted/edited full review
  gh api "repos/kibarik/po-helper/issues/$pr/comments" --jq '[.[] | select(.user.login=="qodo-code-review[bot]") | select(.body|test("Code Review by Qodo"))] | last | .body' \
    | grep -oE "🐞 Bugs? \([0-9]+\)|Rule violations \([0-9]+\)|[0-9]+\.[[:space:]]+[A-Za-z][^<]{3,50}(Bug|Correctness|Maintainability)" | head -12
done

content.text: Multiple review comments exist per PR. Let me identify the most-recently-updated full review and its findings precisely.

input.command:
for pr in 41 37 35; do
  echo "===== PR #$pr ====="
  gh api "repos/kibarik/po-helper/issues/$pr/comments" --jq '.[] | select(.user.login=="qodo-code-review[bot]") | select(.body|test("Code Review by Qodo")) | "\(.updated_at)\tid=\(.id)\t\((.body|capture("🐞 Bugs? \\((?<n>[0-9]+)\\)").n))"' 2>&1
done
input.description: List qodo review comments with update time and bug count

content.text: After re-review, qodo still reports open bugs: #41 →1, #37 →2, #35 →1. These are the updated comments. I need to read what remains — my fixes may not have fully satisfied them, or they're new findings. Let me extract the current finding details.

input.command:
for pr_id in "41:4916621491" "37:4916600143" "35:4916600320"; do
  pr=${pr_id%%:*}; cid=${pr_id##*:}
  echo "########## PR #$pr ##########"
  gh api "repos/kibarik/po-helper/issues/comments/$cid" --jq '.body' \
   | sed -n '/Code Review by Qodo/,$p' \
   | grep -iE "^<summary>|Description|<b><i>|blob/.*#L|pull/.*R[0-9]" \
   | sed -E 's/<[^>]+>//g; s/^\s+//' | grep -vE '^$' | head -40
  echo ""
done
input.description: Extract remaining finding text per PR

result.output:

########## PR #37 ##########
  1.  --ref shift crash ✓ Resolved 🐞 Bug ≡ Correctness
>Description
>install.sh --ref (without a value) will exit early because --ref performs an inner shift and
>the loop always does another unconditional shift under set -e.
>This makes a typo/missing argument a hard failure instead of cleanly defaulting to main or
>[install.sh[R28-37]](https://github.com/kibarik/po-helper/pull/37/files#diff-043df5bdbf6639d7a77e1d44c5226fd7371e5259a1e4df3a0dd5d64c30dca44fR28-R37)
>The argument loop shifts twice for --ref (--ref) shift; ...) and then shifts again after the
>case; with set -e, shift with no remaining args terminates the script.
> [install.sh[19-37]](https://github.com/kibarik/po-helper/blob/d4c6e521e4fb442b3d69d6f3fdd47e864590f041/install.sh/#L19-L37)
>### Issue description
  2.  Report can abort early ✓ Resolved 🐞 Bug ☼ Reliability
>Description
>Phase 5 computes skills_count/commands_count via find | wc -l pipelines under `set -euo
>pipefail; if find` fails (missing/unreadable directory), the script can terminate before printing
>the === PAF-INSTALL-REPORT === block.
>[install.sh[R332-356]](https://github.com/kibarik/po-helper/pull/37/files#diff-043df5bdbf6639d7a77e1d44c5226fd7371e5259a1e4df3a0dd5d64c30dca44fR332-R356)
>The script enables set -euo pipefail and later runs find ... | wc -l inside command
>substitution; with pipefail, find non-zero propagates and can trigger set -e before emitting
> [install.sh[19-20]](https://github.com/kibarik/po-helper/blob/d4c6e521e4fb442b3d69d6f3fdd47e864590f041/install.sh/#L19-L20)
> [install.sh[332-356]](https://github.com/kibarik/po-helper/blob/d4c6e521e4fb442b3d69d6f3fdd47e864590f041/install.sh/#L332-L356)
>### Issue description
  3.  Update blocks config refresh 🐞 Bug ≡ Correctness
>Description
>In --update, the rsync PROTECT list unconditionally excludes .mcp.json, .serena/project.yml,
>and GROUND/**, so framework updates to those files can never be applied even when they are still
>[install.sh[R122-146]](https://github.com/kibarik/po-helper/pull/37/files#diff-043df5bdbf6639d7a77e1d44c5226fd7371e5259a1e4df3a0dd5d64c30dca44fR122-R146)
>install.sh excludes these paths on every --update run, while the included design spec explicitly
>describes conditional protection for .mcp.json, .serena/project.yml, and GROUND/** (and
>mentions protecting .claude/settings.json).
> [install.sh[122-147]](https://github.com/kibarik/po-helper/blob/d4c6e521e4fb442b3d69d6f3fdd47e864590f041/install.sh/#L122-L147)
> [docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-paf-install-full-ecosystem-design.md[59-73]](https://github.com/kibarik/po-helper/blob/d4c6e521e4fb442b3d69d6f3fdd47e864590f041/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-paf-install-full-ecosystem-design.md/#L59-L73)
>### Issue description
  4.  Unprompted remote bootstraps 🐞 Bug ⛨ Security
>Description
>Phase 3 automatically performs networked installs (curl ... | bash, npm i -g, bun add -g)
>It also conflicts with the new /paf-install guardrail that says not to run global installs without
>[install.sh[R163-225]](https://github.com/kibarik/po-helper/pull/37/files#diff-043df5bdbf6639d7a77e1d44c5226fd7371e5259a1e4df3a0dd5d64c30dca44fR163-R225)
>The script runs curl ... | bash and global package installs automatically in Phase 3, while the
> [install.sh[163-225]](https://github.com/kibarik/po-helper/blob/d4c6e521e4fb442b3d69d6f3fdd47e864590f041/install.sh/#L163-L225)
> [.claude/skills/paf-install/SKILL.md[112-116]](https://github.com/kibarik/po-helper/blob/d4c6e521e4fb442b3d69d6f3fdd47e864590f041/.claude/skills/paf-install/SKILL.md/#L112-L116)

########## PR #35 ##########
  1.  node_id collision risk 🐞 Bug ≡ Correctness
>Description
>make_node_id() строит node_id только из (nexus, title), поэтому одинаковые заголовки в одном
>[sa_documentation/cindex_ids.py[R34-39]](https://github.com/kibarik/po-helper/pull/35/files#diff-c757b935d991a80ca38f8f349462d3568d872b8b3994c77a8871a62dffdf360cR34-R39)

content.text:
Now I have the full post-re-review picture:

- **#41**: 3 of 4 findings ✓ Resolved. **1 remaining** — a *new* trivial nit: `bft-validate.md:68` says `hard_gates.md` while line 27 says `resources/hard_gates.md`. Easy fix.
- **#37**: findings 1&2 ✓ Resolved. **2 remaining** — both *design/behavior* (unconditional rsync PROTECT excludes vs spec; Phase-3 auto `curl|bash`/`npm -g` vs paf-install guardrail). Pre-existing installer behavior.
- **#35**: finding 2 ✓ Resolved. **1 remaining** — the `node_id` collision (make_node_id uses title+nexus, not source_page) I deferred earlier; qodo re-flagged it.

Let me fix #41's trivial nit now, and investigate the #35/#37 remaining findings to decide fix vs defer.

content.text: Those files live on the #35 branch, not this one. Let me commit #41's final fix first (it's now fully resolved), then switch branches for #35/#37.

input.command:
git add .claude/commands/bft-validate.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(bft-validate): единый путь ресурса гейтов resources/hard_gates.md

qodo #3 (new): строка «Прогон по таблице из hard_gates.md» противоречила
ссылке resources/hard_gates.md выше по документу. Приведено к единому виду.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push origin fix/bft-writer-doc-style 2>&1 | tail -3
